name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: It’s never been a good bet to bet against America.   And it still isn’t.   #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: We have stared into an abyss of insurrection and autocracy — of pandemic and pain — and “We the People” did not flinch.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: And if we do, then we will meet the central challenge of the age by proving that democracy is durable and strong.   The autocrats will not win the future.   America will.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: We’re vaccinating the nation. We’re creating hundreds of thousands of jobs. We’re delivering real results people can see and feel in their own lives.   #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: Can our democracy deliver on its promise that all of us – created equal in the image of God – have a chance to lead lives of dignity, respect, and possibility?  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: And if we are to truly restore the soul of America – we need to protect the sacred right to vote.   More people voted in the last presidential election than ever before   in our history – in the middle of one of the worst pandemics ever.    #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: Immigrants have done so much for America during the pandemic – as they have throughout our history.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: Talk to most responsible gun owners, most hunters – they’ll tell you there’s no possible justification for having 100 rounds – 100 bullets – in a weapon.   #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: In the 1990s, we passed universal background checks and a ban on assault weapons and high—capacity magazines that hold 100 rounds that can be fired in seconds.   We beat the NRA. Mass shootings and gun violence declined.   #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: And I need not tell anyone this, but gun violence is an epidemic in America.   #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: It’s estimated that more than 50 women are shot and killed by an intimate partner – every month in America.   Pass it and save lives.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: Let’s reauthorize the Violence Against Women Act, which has been law in this country for 27 years since I first wrote it.   It will close the so—called “boyfriend” loophole to keep guns out of the hands of abusers.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: Most men and women in uniform wear their badge and serve their communities honorably.   I know them. I know they want to help meet this moment as well.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: And my fellow Americans, we must come together to heal the soul of this nation.   #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: We went to Afghanistan to get the terrorists who attacked us on 9/11.    We delivered justice to Osama Bin Laden and we degraded the terrorist threat of al Qaeda in Afghanistan.   After 20 years of American valor and sacrifice, it’s time to bring our troops home.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: America is an idea – unique in the world.   We are all created equal. It’s who we are. We cannot walk away from that principle.   #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: America will stand up to unfair trade practices that undercut American workers and industries, like subsidies for state-owned enterprises and the theft of American technologies and intellectual property.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: The investments I’ve proposed tonight also advance a foreign policy that benefits the middle class.   That means making sure every nation plays by the same rules in the global economy, including China.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: --from China and Russia to India and the European Union in my first 100 days.   I wanted the world to see that there is consensus that we are at an inflection point in history.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: That’s why – I kept my commitment to rejoin the Paris Climate Agreement on my first day in office.    And I kept my commitment to convene a climate summit right here in America, with all of the major economies of the world –

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: There’s no wall high enough to keep any virus away.   #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: My fellow Americans, we have to show not just that we are back, but that we are here to stay.   And that we aren’t going it alone – we’re going to be leading with our allies.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: My fellow Americans, trickle-down economics has   never worked.   It’s time to grow the economy from the bottom up and middle-out.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: 20 million Americans lost their jobs in the pandemic – working- and middle-class Americans.   At the same time, the roughly 650 Billionaires in America saw their net worth increase by more than $1 Trillion.   #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: According to one study, CEOs make 320 times what their average workers make.   The pandemic has only made things worse.

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: Instead of using the tax savings to raise wages and invest in research and development – it poured billions of dollars into the pockets of CEOs.    In fact, the pay gap between CEOs and their workers is now among the largest in history.

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: Look at the big tax cut in 2017.   It was supposed to pay for itself and generate vast economic growth.    Instead it added $2 trillion to the deficit.   It was a huge windfall for corporate America and those at the very top.

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: When you hear someone say that they don’t want to raise taxes on the wealthiest 1% and on corporate America – ask them: whose taxes are you going to raise instead, and whose are you going to cut?

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: It raises the revenue to pay for the plans I’ve proposed that will create millions of jobs and grow the economy. #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: But I will not add to the tax burden of the middle class of this country.    They’re already paying enough.   What I’ve proposed is fair. It’s fiscally responsible.   #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: We’re going to get rid of the loopholes that allow Americans who make more than $1 million a year pay a lower rate on their capital gains than working Americans pay on their work.    This will only affect three tenths of 1% of all Americans.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: And, we’re going to reward work, not wealth.   #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: A lot of companies evade taxes through tax havens from Switzerland to Bermuda to the Cayman Islands.   And they benefit from tax loopholes and deductions that allow for offshoring jobs and shifting profits overseas.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: A recent study shows that 55 of the nation’s biggest corporations paid zero in federal income tax last year.   No federal taxes on more than $40 billion in profits.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: It’s time for corporate America and the wealthiest 1% of Americans to pay their fair share.   Just pay their fair share.   #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: Let’s start with what I will not do.   I will not impose any tax increases on people making less than $400,000 a year.    #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: Let’s give Medicare the power to save hundreds of billions of dollars by negotiating lower prices for prescription drugs.    That won’t just help people on Medicare – it will lower prescription drug costs for everyone.   #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: The Affordable Care Act has been a lifeline for millions of Americans –protecting people with pre-existing conditions, protecting women’s health.   #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: In March we expanded a tax credit for every child in a family.   Up to a $3,000 Child Tax Credit for children over 6 — and $3,600 for children under 6.    With two parents, two kids, that’s up to $7,200 in your pocket to help take care of your family.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: Third, the American Families Plan will finally provide up to 12 weeks of paid family and medical leave.   No one should have to choose between a job and paycheck or taking care of themselves and a loved one – a parent, spouse, or child.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: But, the rest of the world isn’t waiting for us. Doing nothing is not an option.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: “Wall Street didn’t build this country. The middle class built this country. And unions built the middle class.” #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: “The Americans Jobs Plan is a blue-collar blueprint to build America.” #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: Nearly 90% of the infrastructure jobs created in the American Jobs Plan don’t require a college degree. 75% don’t require an associate’s degree. #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: Independent experts estimate the American Jobs Plan will add millions of jobs and trillions of dollars in economic growth for years to come. These are good-paying jobs that can’t be outsourced.  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: And all the investments in American jobs right now--we got them all on one principle #BuyAmerican  #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: American jobs plan will help millions of people get back to their jobs and back to their careers #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: Public investment in infrastructure has literally transformed America. Our attitudes as well as our opportunities.   #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: More jobs in the first 100 days than any President on record. #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: We're on track to cut childhood poverty in half this year. #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: We’re vaccinating the nation. We’re creating hundreds of thousands of jobs. We’re delivering real results people can see and feel in their own lives. Opening the doors of opportunity. Guaranteeing fairness and justice. #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: We're working, again. Dreaming again. Discovering again. And, leading the world again. #JointAddress

name: Rep. Brendan Boyle
tweet: Now—after just 100 days—I can report to the nation: America is on the move again. Turning peril into possibility. Crisis into opportunity. Setback into strength.
